location.college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology, Allahabad
location.nearest_major_hospital: United Medicity / government hospitals in Prayagraj area
location.technology_ecosystem: Prayagraj academic and public-sector ecosystem; limited large private technology-park exposure compared with major metros
location.data_origin: Official address; distances and coordinates are map-derived estimates requiring confirmation

senior_junior_culture.summary: Public evidence suggests active peer and club interaction, but the available source set is too weak for a college-wide conclusion.
negative_themes: club hierarchy and batch interaction require direct verification

academic_culture.summary: Frequently described as academically intensive and technology-focused.
positive_themes: strong IT/ECE curriculum and peer coding environment

hostel_reality.summary: Officially residential with eight hostel buildings; lived experience on mess, room condition and curfew varies and needs recent first-person verification.
negative_themes: mess and room quality may vary

club_and_campus_life.summary: Official Gymkhana and technical communities indicate broad activity, but current club-by-club activity was not fully verified.
positive_themes: music, dance, drama, sports, literary, robotics and developer communities
club_and_campus_life.evidence_strength: Moderate official signal; limited student signal

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: Repeated public discussion and strong placement outcomes indicate a competitive software/coding environment, while access differs by programme and recruiter.
positive_themes: competitive programming, development and strong software recruiter history
negative_themes: branch-specific eligibility is not transparent in public data

locality_and_student_life.summary: The campus is in Jhalwa, outside the central city area but near the airport; travel estimates require map confirmation.
positive_themes: residential campus and lower living costs
negative_themes: less spontaneous metro/startup exposure than major technology cities

overall_satisfaction.summary: Best suited to students prioritising IT/ECE, coding peers and placements; students prioritising a central-city lifestyle or low workload should verify fit carefully.

parent_mode_summary.summary: Identity, seat matrix, historical cutoffs and NIRF placement cohorts are well sourced. Verify the 2026 fee circular, total residential cost, scholarship cycle and hostel rules before admission.

student_mode_summary.summary: Strong fit for students prioritising IT/ECE, coding peers and software placements. Verify branch workload, recruiter eligibility and current hostel/club reality.

decision_risks.basis: Detailed 2026 fee circular was not independently extracted; current calculations use the 2025-batch schedule.
NIRF cohort records and placement-office registered-student statistics are not directly interchangeable.
Students seeking software roles from ECE should verify current company eligibility and workload.
Some detailed hostel rules are from an older manual; current curfew and allotment need confirmation.
Student-experience evidence is mainly public search-level material and should not be generalized.

data_gaps: Current authoritative NAAC grade/validity and programme-wise NBA validity were not verified.
Mess charges are variable and the official 2026 fee schedule states they are payable as applicable; no mess total is estimated.
Current hostel room allocation, availability guarantee and exact timings require confirmation.
Recruiter roles, branch eligibility, CGPA cut-offs and packages are generally not published in the verified source.
Qualitative evidence is search-level and needs thread/video-level manual review before generalisation.
Current club activity and recruitment cycles require direct verification.

conflicts.source_2_value: Placement brochure: 431 registered, 422 placed, average ₹33 LPA, highest ₹120 LPA
conflicts.possible_reason: NIRF graduating-cohort definition and placement-office registered-participant definition differ.
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Retain both with explicit scope; do not merge percentages or package statistics.

conflicts.source_1_value: JoSAA profile: 419 of 431 who opted were placed
conflicts.source_2_value: Placement brochure: 422 of 431 registered were placed
conflicts.possible_reason: Documents may use different cut-off dates, inclusion rules, or correction versions.
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Ask the placement office which figure is final and preserve both until confirmed.

conflicts.possible_reason: Year-on-year ranking movement and different publication years.
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Display by year; do not treat as a same-year contradiction.

conflicts.source_1_value: Approximately 2,000 residents across eight hostel buildings
conflicts.possible_reason: One figure is total residential scope and the other is girls-only capacity.
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Preserve scope labels and manually verify current occupancy/capacity.

manual_verification_required: Confirm NAAC and programme-wise NBA records on the issuing portals.
Confirm mess advance/actual charges for the 2026 batch.
Reconcile the 419 versus 422 placement count for the 431-student registered/opted cohort.
Confirm current hostel entitlement, room sharing, timings and grievance response.
Confirm recruiter-specific roles, branch eligibility, CGPA requirements and package components.
Confirm scholarship deadlines, allocated slots and renewal requirements for 2026-27.
Open and assess individual recent Reddit/YouTube/student sources before publishing qualitative claims.
Confirm current club activity and first-year recruitment processes.
